regular_savings.free_atm_transactions: 5 per month at own ATMs, 3 at other bank ATMs

horizon_classic.minimum_due: 5% of total outstanding or ₹200 whichever is higher

emi_conversion.processing_fee: 1% of transaction amount or ₹199 whichever is higher

horizon_platinum.minimum_due: 5% of total outstanding or ₹200 whichever is higher
horizon_platinum.reward_points: 2 points per ₹100 spent, 5x on dining and travel

emi_conversion.processing_fee: 0.75% of transaction amount or ₹149 whichever is higher

credit_cards.billing_cycle: Monthly (statement date on 15th of each month, due date on 5th of next month)
credit_cards.billing_note: No interest charged if FULL outstanding is paid by due date. Interest applies from transaction date if minimum due only is paid.

home_loan.interest_rate: 8.50% to 9.25% per annum (floating, linked to RLLR)
home_loan.processing_fee: 0.50% of loan amount or ₹10,000 whichever is higher
home_loan.ltv_ratio: Up to 90% for loans up to ₹30 Lakhs, 80% above

car_loan.max_amount: 100% of ex-showroom price for new cars

mobile_app.name: HorizonPay
mobile_app.upi_daily_limit: ₹1 Lakh per transaction, ₹2 Lakhs per day

atm.own_bank_atm: 5 free transactions per month, ₹20 per transaction after limit
atm.other_bank_atm: 3 free transactions per month (metro), 5 (non-metro), ₹21 per transaction after

dispute_and_grievance.unauthorized_transaction_reporting_windo: Within 3 days for zero liability; 4-7 days for limited liability as per RBI guidelines

faqs.q: How is credit card interest calculated?
faqs.a: If you pay only the minimum due, interest is charged on the entire outstanding balance from the transaction date (not the statement date) at the monthly rate. For Horizon Classic card, this is 3.49% per month. For example, on ₹31,800 outstanding, if you pay ₹4,200 minimum due, interest will be approximately ₹31,800 × 3.49% = ₹1,109.82 for the next month on the unpaid amount.

faqs.q: Can I convert my credit card outstanding to EMI?
faqs.a: Yes. Outstanding balances above ₹2,500 can be converted to EMI. For Classic card: 3-month EMI at 13% p.a., 6/9-month at 14%, 12-month at 15%, 18-month at 15.5%, 24-month at 16%. Processing fee: 1% or ₹199 whichever is higher. Call 1800-XXX-XXXX or use HorizonPay app to convert.

faqs.q: What happens if I don't pay my credit card bill?
faqs.a: If you miss the due date: a late payment fee of ₹600 (for outstanding above ₹10,000) is charged. Interest at 3.49%/month accrues on the full outstanding from transaction date. Your credit score will also be negatively impacted after 30 days of non-payment.

faqs.q: How do I report a fraudulent transaction?
faqs.a: Report immediately by calling 1800-XXX-XXXX (24x7) or blocking your card via HorizonPay app. Under RBI guidelines, if reported within 3 days, your liability is zero. If reported in 4-7 days, liability is capped at ₹10,000. File a complaint at the branch and get a written acknowledgment. Dispute is resolved within 7-10 working days.

faqs.q: How to update KYC / change name or address?
faqs.a: Visit the nearest branch with original + self-attested copies of: new address/identity proof (Aadhaar, Passport etc.). For name change after marriage: marriage certificate + updated Aadhaar/Passport required. You can also initiate KYC update via net banking for address change using Aadhaar OTP-based verification.

faqs.q: What is the interest-free period on credit cards?
faqs.a: Up to 50 days from the date of purchase if you pay the FULL outstanding amount by the due date. No interest-free period if you carry forward any balance from the previous month.

faqs.q: How to activate/unblock my net banking?
faqs.a: Visit the nearest Horizon Bank branch with valid ID proof. Alternatively, you can reset via the HorizonPay app using your registered mobile number and debit card details. Customer care at 1800-XXX-XXXX can also guide you through the process.
